What Is MOQ in Clothing Manufacturing? The Complete 2026 Guide

✎ FABRIOZA Team 📅 July 1, 2026 ☕ 10 min read
Home / Blog / What Is MOQ in Clothing Manufacturing? The Complete 2026 Guide

MOQ is the three-letter acronym that determines whether a clothing startup can launch or not. Understanding how Minimum Order Quantity works — and why it varies so dramatically between factories — is essential for every brand owner. This guide explains everything, including how FABRIOZA achieves 50-piece MOQs when most factories require 300-500 pieces.

MOQ Definition

MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) is the smallest number of units a manufacturer will produce in a single order. It exists because of fixed setup costs: pattern making, machine calibration, fabric sourcing, and operator training. These costs are the same whether making 50 pieces or 5,000.

Why Factories Have High MOQs

Traditional manufacturers spread setup costs across large quantities to remain profitable. A factory in China might need to order 500 meters of fabric minimum per color — justifying 300-500 unit MOQs to use that fabric efficiently.

Why FABRIOZA Achieves 50pcs MOQ

Sialkot's local fabric supply chain is the key. Fabric mills are within 50km, enabling smaller purchases without surplus charges. Combined with efficient production systems and lower operating costs, we can profitably manufacture 50-piece orders.

Types of MOQ

  • Per Style MOQ: Minimum per garment design (our 50-piece minimum)
  • Per Color MOQ: Some factories require minimums per colorway
  • Per Order MOQ: Total minimum across all styles in one order

MOQ at Different Factory Types

Factory TypeTypical MOQBest For
Large China factory300-1,000 pcsEstablished brands, high volume
Small China factory100-300 pcsMid-size brands
Bangladesh500-2,000 pcsVolume basics
Turkey200-500 pcsEU brands
FABRIOZA (Pakistan)50 pcsStartups, small brands

How MOQ Affects Your Budget

At 50 pieces, a hoodie costs ~$18/unit = $900 total. At 200 pieces, ~$15/unit = $3,000 total. The per-unit savings are real, but the cash required is 3x higher. For startups, lower total investment usually matters more than per-unit cost.

How to Negotiate MOQ

  • Start with 50 pieces to test demand
  • Commit to a second order to secure lower pricing
  • Use the same fabric across styles to combine fabric orders
  • Build a relationship — loyal clients get flexibility

FABRIOZA's 50pcs MOQ Explained

Our 50-piece MOQ is real and applies across all product categories. You can mix sizes and up to 3 colors within that 50-piece minimum. No hidden fees. No surcharges for small orders. The same ISO 9001 quality process applies regardless of quantity.

FABRIOZA's 50pcs MOQ Is Real

No minimum fees. No surcharges. Start with 50 pieces and scale. Free quote in 24 hours.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is the 50pcs MOQ per color or per style?

Per style. You can split across up to 3 colors (minimum 15-20 pieces per color).

Can I order less than 50 pieces?

50 pieces is our firm minimum for bulk production. For samples, we produce single pieces for approval.

Does MOQ include multiple sizes?

Yes, you can distribute your 50 pieces across all available sizes.

Why is MOQ higher for some products?

Complex garments (like tracksuits with multiple components) may have higher MOQs due to setup complexity. Contact us for specific product MOQs.

Can MOQ be reduced for repeat orders?

For established clients with proven designs, we offer flexibility on MOQ for repeat orders.
